coding_notes: Gemini was originally truncated; re-run at higher max_tokens provided full 6-section coverage. cns3 (pentostatin-like ADA inhibitor co-expressed in C. militaris) is a known component of the natural cordycepin biosynthetic gene cluster and is essential for self-protection in the native producer. Whether each vendor names it is a load-bearing mechanism-correctness check. Verdicts below reflect the gemini re-run.
ground_truth_notes: Established knowledge: cns3 is a deaminase-domain protein in the Cordyceps militaris cordycepin BGC that confers self-resistance by inhibiting adenosine deaminase (ADA). The natural pathway is cns1+cns2+cns3 (Xia et al. 2017 Cell Chem Biol; Wang et al. 2020 metabolic engineering). Heterologous cordycepin expression in non-Cordyceps hosts requires cns3 (or equivalent host engineering) for stable production.

claims.claim_text: cns1+cns2 cytosolic pathway does NOT compete with uricase + lactoferrin for ER chaperones (no PDI/ERO1 conflict).
Names cordycepin self-toxicity (RNA chain terminator / actively kills host) as a major host-viability risk.

gemini.value_quoted: 'High-flux cytotoxic small molecule synthesis...actively kills the host'

claims.claim_text: Names cns3 (pentostatin-like ADA inhibitor) as essential / non-negotiable for stable cordycepin co-expression. (CRITICAL MECHANISM)

deepseek.value_quoted: Names ADA-mediated 3'-deoxyinosine conversion as a purity risk; does NOT name cns3 as the solution. Suggests ADA knockout or resistant pol as engineering options.

gemini.value_quoted: Names endogenous A. oryzae ADAs and 3'-deoxyinosine as purity issue; does NOT name cns3

openai.value_quoted: Names ADA conversion as the dominant risk; does NOT name cns3.

anthropic.value_quoted: EXPLICIT: 'C. militaris solves this by co-expressing cns3, a pentostatin-like ADA inhibitor...if you express cns1+cns2 without cns3, you get the worst of both worlds...cns3 is non-negotiable'

claims.claim_text: Specifies a cordycepin titer estimate with assumptions and a published sanity-check from a different heterologous host.

deepseek.value_quoted: 5-20 mg/L base; cites Xia 2017 yeast 0.2 mg/L, E. coli 0.1-0.8 g/L, Y. lipolytica >3 g/L

gemini.value_quoted: <50 mg/L; cites Qin 2020 Metab Eng S. cerevisiae ~168 mg/L

openai.value_quoted: Discusses titer-related metabolic risks but does not give specific titer estimate

anthropic.value_quoted: 50-250 mg/L with cns3; cites Tan 2022 Metab Eng, Yang group; honestly notes 'memory may be imperfect, verify against current Metabolic Engineering literature'

claims.claim_text: Recommends a specific low-cost de-risking experiment (cordycepin tolerance assay).

deepseek.value_quoted: Cordycepin-sensitivity growth assay; <€500; MIC threshold for go/no-go

gemini.value_quoted: Discusses growth-defect vs induced-culture observations but does not specify cordycepin tolerance assay as a separate experiment

openai.value_quoted: (focuses on adenosine feeding / extraction questions instead)

anthropic.value_quoted: Cordycepin sensitivity assay; <$500; 3 IC50 thresholds for go/cns3-required/abandon decisions

claims.claim_text: Pushes back on the proposal: recommends parallel mono-cassette strains instead of single co-expression strain.

gemini.value_quoted: 'Develop two parallel, specialized strains'; Strain A optimized for protein + Strain B optimized for cordycepin

openai.value_quoted: 'Would not make the triple-function strain the primary development path'; recommends mono-cassette cordycepin strain first

anthropic.value_quoted: Implied via 'switch host (Y. lipolytica, S. cerevisiae) for cordycepin and keep A. oryzae for the uricase+lactoferrin cassette'
